text: If you want to know your past life, look into your present condition. If you want to know your future life, look at your present actions.
author: Padmasambhava

text: Although my view is higher than the sky, my respect for the cause and effect of actions is as fine as grains of flour.
author: Padmasambhava

text: Do not encumber your mind with useless thoughts. What good is it to think of what has already been, or of what is yet to be?
author: Milarepa

text: All worldly pursuits end in sorrow. Renounce them and be happy.
author: Milarepa

text: When you run after your thoughts, you are lost. Let them pass by like clouds in the sky.
author: Milarepa

text: In the gap between two thoughts, thought-free wakefulness continuously shines.
author: Longchenpa
source: Finding Rest in the Nature of Mind

text: Since everything is but an apparition, perfect in being what it is, having nothing to do with good or bad, acceptance or rejection, one might as well burst out laughing.
author: Longchenpa

text: Friends, the things you desire give no more satisfaction than drinking sea water; therefore practice contentment.
author: Atisha

text: All the joy the world contains has come through wishing happiness for others. All the misery the world contains has come through wanting pleasure for oneself.
author: Shantideva

text: If the problem can be solved, why worry? If the problem cannot be solved, worrying will do you no good.
author: Shantideva

text: Be kind whenever possible. It is always possible.
author: Dalai Lama

text: If you want others to be happy, practice compassion. If you want to be happy, practice compassion.
author: Dalai Lama

text: My religion is very simple. My religion is kindness.
author: Dalai Lama

text: Sitting quietly, doing nothing, spring comes and the grass grows by itself.
author: Dogen

text: To study Buddhism is to study the self. To study the self is to forget the self. To forget the self is to be enlightened by all things.
author: Dogen

text: If you cannot find the truth right where you are, where else do you expect to find it?
author: Dogen

text: The whole moon and the entire sky are reflected in one dewdrop on the grass.
author: Dogen

text: If you love the holy and hate the ordinary, you float and sink in the ocean of birth and death.
author: Linji Yixuan

text: Followers of the Way, the Dharma of the Buddhas calls for no special undertakings. Just act ordinary, without trying to do anything particular.
author: Linji Yixuan

text: There is no place in Buddhism for using effort. Just be ordinary and nothing special.
author: Linji Yixuan

text: The Buddha is your own mind. Do not look for him outside your own nature.
author: Huineng

text: If you use your mind to study reality, you will understand neither reality nor the mind. If you study reality without using your mind, you will understand both.
author: Bodhidharma

text: The great way is not difficult for those who have no preferences. When love and hate are both absent everything becomes clear and undisguised.
author: Sengcan

text: Before enlightenment, chop wood, carry water. After enlightenment, chop wood, carry water.
author: Huihai

text: If you have time to breathe, you have time to meditate. You breathe when you walk. You breathe when you stand. You breathe when you lie down.
author: Ajahn Chah

text: The heart is the only book worth reading.
author: Ajahn Chah

text: Do not try to become anything. Do not make yourself into anything. Do not be a meditator. Do not become enlightened. When you sit, let it be.
author: Ajahn Chah

text: If you let go a little, you will have a little peace. If you let go a lot, you will have a lot of peace.
author: Ajahn Chah

text: The Dhamma is not far away. It's right here, in us, around us. It's in the trees, the water, the air. We're swimming in the Dhamma.
author: Ajahn Chah

text: Truth is something you cannot find anywhere else except in your own heart. You must look there.
author: Buddhadasa Bhikkhu

text: Nothing whatsoever should be grasped at or clung to. This is the whole of the Dhamma in a nutshell.
author: Buddhadasa Bhikkhu

text: Nature is always teaching us Dhamma. When we look at a tree, we see that the tree doesn't cling to its leaves.
author: Buddhadasa Bhikkhu

text: The entire cosmos is a cooperative. The sun, the moon, and the stars live together as a cooperative.
author: Buddhadasa Bhikkhu

text: Peace is within oneself to be found in the same place as agitation. Not somewhere else. Peace is not found in a forest or on a hilltop, nor is it given by a teacher.
author: Ajahn Sumedho

text: Let it be. Let it go. This is not about becoming anything. This is about unbecoming everything that isn't really you.
author: Ajahn Sumedho

text: The only thing we really have is nowness, is now.
author: Ajahn Sumedho
